title: orchis angustifolia subsp. russowii
intro: Orchis angustifolia subsp. russowii, also known as Russow's Orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Orchis, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Russow's Orchid include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
